Comprehensive Biography for Wang Bi

sections.biography-source-undefined:
Wang Bi, styled Fusi, was from Shandong commandery. He was a younger son of Wang Ye and his paternal grandmother was the daughter of Liu Biao.

He was described as a man who did not feel comfortable socializing.

Wang Bi was Gentleman of the Secretariat. After Cao Shuang was executed by Sima Yi, Wang Bi was removed from his post. That autumn, he fell seriously ill and died. He was only twenty-three years old. He was childless.

Wang Bi is most recognized for his commentaries on Laozi and the Book of Changes, which have influenced Chinese philosophy by some degree.

full_text:
Wang Bi (Fusi) 王弼 (輔嗣)
ARomance of the Three KingdomsEncyclopedia officer profile.
Wang Bi (王弼), styledFusi (輔嗣), was born in Shanyang. A noted, if eccentric, philosopher central in the school of mystics. Wang Bi was affiliated with the Wei Kingdom. Wang Bi lived during the Three Kingdoms period of Chinese history.
